prompt:
Three checkout escalations landed on me this sprint. They may or may not be connected, but they're all in the session/pricing/refund code in this directory and I can reproduce every one of them.

1) Promo pricing goes stale the moment a shopper edits their cart. Apply WELCOME5 to a qualifying cart, then change a quantity — the discount stays whatever it was at apply time, including staying applied after the cart drops below the $40 minimum that promo is supposed to require. Support also caught a genuinely weird variant: a $50 cart got $1.10 off with SAVE10 moments after a different shopper had used SAVE10 on an $11 cart. Ten percent of fifty dollars is not $1.10.

2) Finance's ledger export rejects some of our charges because the amount isn't a whole number of cents — their log shows values like 4159.960000000001 in the cents column. Every rejected order had a promo on it; orders without a promo come out as clean integers every single time.

3) A customer was refunded twice for one return. The agent's first refund click sat waiting on a slow gateway response, they clicked again, and both went through — the order now shows more refunded than was ever paid. Refunds done one after another behave correctly; it's specifically the overlapping ones.

node --test test_checkout.ts fails and covers all of the above. Get it passing by fixing the checkout code — test_checkout.ts is the contract and stays exactly as it is.
